title: Cops & Robbers

chord_progression.chord_stab_style: Quick rhythmic stabs — G# on beat 1, rapid E and B movement on beats 2–4, not slow sustained chords
chord_progression.theory_notes: G# natural minor: G#, A#, B, C#, D#, E, F#. The G# major chord (C in MIDI = B# enharmonically = major 3rd from G#) uses the raised 3rd — this is the Picardy third / harmonic minor borrowing. In G# minor: G#maj=I(borrowed), E=VI(major), B=III(major). The quick stab sequence G#→E→B implies a motion from V to III to I if you shift perspective: these three major chords all share notes and create a bright, driving harmonic loop. The Skepta grime influence pushes toward the darker stab delivery vs. the soulful Kettama approach.

strings_riff.description: Ascending chromatic/arpeggio run that defines the track's melodic identity. Pattern: G→G#→C→D# repeating over 2 octaves. This outlines the Ab(G#) major chord (G# C D# = Ab, C, Eb in flat notation) with a passing G tone below. Strings play this as a rising melodic hook rather than sustained pads — aggressive, forward-moving.

structure.description: Strings 1+2 chord riff, Chord stabs, Acapella vocal starts immediately. Drill FX audio texture. LFO Sweep.
Kick enters with Uplifter building into bar 9. Still no bass — drums and strings only.
Bass enters bar 10 (G#4 root section). Strings 3 adds another layer. Open Hat accent hits every 4 bars. Saw riff enters at bar 9. Downlifter FX shapes the drop energy.
Bass shifts to C#4 root section (more rooted, heavier). Saw continues. Hat pattern consistent.
G#4 bass section returns. Bass riff continues with same rhythmic pattern.
Strings 3 enters for maximum density. All three string layers active. Saw prominent. This is the heaviest section of the track.
Saw carries on, Downlifter FX used between sections for energy modulation.
Vinyl crackle returns (from intro). LFO Sweep re-enters. Uplifter builds. Strings continue but arrangement strips back.
Strings 3 + Saw return at full intensity. Uplifter. Drill FX at transitions.
Saw and Bass continue. Extended run.
Chord track sustains through to end. Strings phase out.

tracks.name: Kick
tracks.role: 2-step pattern — kick on beats 1 and 3 per bar (not 4-on-the-floor). Ghost notes at humanised velocities between downbeats. C4.

tracks.name: Hat
tracks.role: Closed hat on every off-beat (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5 per bar). C4. Constant driving 8th-note pulse.

tracks.name: Rim
tracks.role: Rim shot / clap on beats 2 and 4 (positions 1.0, 3.0 per bar). C4. Snare substitute.

tracks.name: Open Hat
tracks.role: Single open hat accent hit every 4 bars at section downbeats. C4. Used as an ear-catching marker rather than a groove element.

tracks.name: Bass
tracks.role: Main bass — alternates between two pitch centres: G#4 root sections and C#4 root sections. Long held notes with rhythmic subdivision. All notes in G# minor.

tracks.name: Strings 1
tracks.role: Main string riff — ascending G→G#→C→D# arpeggio across 2 octaves. Runs entire track from bar 1.

tracks.name: Strings 2
tracks.role: Second string layer — same ascending riff, layered for thickness.

tracks.name: Strings 3
tracks.role: Third string layer — enters at bar 9 and select peak sections. Maximum string density.

tracks.name: Saw
tracks.role: Saw synth playing same ascending riff. Adds edge and brightness to the string texture. Enters at bar 9.

tracks.name: Chord
tracks.role: Chord stabs — G#maj→Emaj→Bmaj quick progression. Quick rhythmic hits rather than sustained chords. Runs throughout.

tracks.name: Crash
tracks.role: Crash hits at 2-bar intervals (every even bar from bar 11) — constant rhythmic marker

tracks.name: Vinyl
tracks.role: Vinyl crackle texture — intro (bars 1–9) and break (bars 49–57)

tracks.name: LFO Sweep
tracks.role: LFO modulation sweep — intro and break sections. Creates wobbly, filter-sweep effect.

tracks.name: Drill FX
tracks.role: Drill/metal FX hits at bar transitions (bars 1–3, 9–11, 13–15, etc.) — grime-influenced texture

tracks.name: Downlifter
tracks.role: Falling sweep — used after drop peaks to signal energy change (bars 17–45 area)

kick.pattern: 2-step: beats 1 and 3 (positions 0.0, 2.0 per bar) in 2-bar phrase. Ghost notes between — velocity humanised.

bass_pattern.key_notes: G# = root (1st), E = major 6th (VI of G# minor), C# = minor 4th (iv of G# minor)
bass_pattern.description: Bass alternates between two-note root sections: clips with G#4 and E4 alternating (bars 10–24, 25–31) and single-root C#4 clips (bar 17–24, 32+). The G#→E movement in the first section creates a pendulum between root and major 6th — similar to how MPH Raw uses E→F phrygian tension, but softer. Long held notes (1.88 beats) with smaller fills (1.35 beats). All in G# minor.
bass_pattern.technique: Long held basses (nearly 2 beats each) create a smooth, legato bass feel rather than punchy stabs. This gives the track its 'slow burner' rolling quality despite the 2-step kick.

key_techniques: 2-step kick (beats 1+3 only) vs. 4-on-the-floor — gives a more garage/grime feel than house
Ascending string arpeggio as melodic hook — G→G#→C→D# identifies the track immediately
Triple string layering (Strings 1+2+3) for maximum harmonic density at peak sections
Chord stabs as rhythmic device, not harmonic backdrop — quick G#→E→B in 4 beats
Drill FX at bar transitions — grime influence adds aggression
Vinyl crackle in intro and break — roots the sound in UK dance culture
Open Hat used as section marker (every 4 bars) rather than groove element — architectural use
theory_notes: Cops & Robbers operates in G# minor at 136 BPM. The harmonic material (G# major, E major, B major) creates a bright, aggressive minor key sound using borrowed major chords throughout. Analytically: G# minor gives you i(G#m), III(B), iv(C#m), VI(E), VII(F#). By using G# major (the Picardy third), E major (VI), and B major (III), the track avoids any actual minor-sounding chords — everything is major, but the G# root makes it feel fundamentally minor. This is a common grime/UK garage technique: major chords over a minor root gives aggression without darkness. Compare to Kettama who uses explicit minor chords (Bbm) for warmth — Cops & Robbers is harder and more confrontational.
vs_mph_raw: MPH Raw: 4-on-the-floor, E minor pedal, phrygian tension, no chords. Cops & Robbers: 2-step kick, G# minor, quick major chord stabs, ascending string riff. Both aggressive but in different ways — MPH is rhythmic aggression, C&R is harmonic aggression.

elements_to_transplant: 2-step kick (beats 1+3) with ghost notes for humanization
Ascending arpeggio string riff as main melodic hook
Quick chord stab sequence (3 chords in 4 beats)
Drill FX at bar transitions for grime texture
